channel.title: Python on Reece Hart
channel.description: Recent content in Python on Reece Hart

item.title: Using virtualenv, pip, and ipython
item.description: This page provides a quick synopsis on how to use three of my favorite Python features together: virtualenv, which provides a standardized mechanism to isolate python environments (including in WSGI setups) pip <http://www.pip-installer.org/>__, which facilitates packages installation in virtualenv environments or otherwise ipython <http://ipython.scipy.org/>__, a terrific interactive shell with readline and debugging support First, you’ll need python, virtualenv, and ipython installed. These are commonly available in most (all?) Linux distributions, or you can install from source.
